body_txt: A Pet the Dog moment is when a character shows kindness to something smaller or weaker. But when the character makes a show of it, usually to bolster their own image, you've got this trope. The act can sometimes be faked, but usually it's genuine, even if it's a meager act. Still, the "dog" usually senses that this is somewhat less than selfless, and can be indifferent, bored, or even offended by the 'kindness.' Can involve a literal photographer, but not always. Similar to Helping Granny Cross the Street , the point is not to show benevolence as much as to appear to show it. If the character is a Villain with Good Publicity , this is a standard PR tactic. If the character is a Hero with Bad Publicity , it's usually an attempt to change their image, but it's rarely ever successful . A subtrope of Pet the Dog and Sister Trope to We Care , Politicians Kiss Babies . Contrast What You Are in the Dark , where someone does a kind act even though no one will see it. May overlap with Rich Kid Turned Social Activist . Can also be a form of Go-Karting with Bowser if the person really wants to cherish this moment.
